short: SGLang's technique for automatically reusing the KV cache of shared prompt prefixes.
definition: RadixAttention is SGLang's algorithm for automatic prefix caching. It stores KV cache entries in a radix tree keyed by token sequence; when a new request shares a prefix with a cached one (a system prompt, a RAG document, prior conversation turns), computation starts from the branch point instead of from scratch. Workloads with heavy prefix overlap see large reductions in time-to-first-token.
